title: Urera expansa
taxonomy: Urera expansa is classified in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family consists of over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are vines or shrubs. Urera expansa is a species of flowering plant in the genus Urera. The genus Urera is native to tropical regions of the world, particularly in Central and South America. The classification of Urera expansa is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta.

intro: Urera expansa, also known as Tahina or Tahina palm, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Urticaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, as well as the potential to treat skin conditions and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Urera expansa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
